checks: Scheduler class exported and extends DurableObject<Env>
Two recurring jobs (heartbeat every 5 min, expiry every 30 min)
Single-alarm multiplexing via next_heartbeat / next_expiry KV keys
nextOccurrence() prevents drift and tight-loop re-fire after outages
All persistent state in ctx.storage — survives eviction and hibernation
initGuard set before first await to prevent concurrent double-init
Constructor limited to idempotent DDL only (lazy-init pattern)
GET /status returns lastHeartbeat (ISO string or null) and activeSessions count
Session expiry uses atomic single-statement DELETE (no TOCTOU race)
new_sqlite_classes used in wrangler — sql.exec requires SQLite backend
Worker default export routes to idFromName('global') stub
compatibility_date >= 2024-04-03 (set to 2025-01-01)
SCHEDULER binding with class_name: Scheduler
Migration tag v1 with new_sqlite_classes: [Scheduler]
main points to durable_object.ts
Covers single-alarm constraint and event-queue pattern
Covers in-memory state loss (hibernation / eviction)
Covers constructor-runs-on-every-wake pitfall
Covers concurrent-request race via initGuard
Covers alarm drift and tight-loop after outage
Covers new_sqlite_classes vs new_classes migration pitfall
Covers atomic SQL DELETE to avoid TOCTOU races
Covers storage-write ordering before setAlarm
File is non-empty and valid JSON
overall_passed is true

notes: Implementation uses a single-alarm event-queue pattern to run two independent recurring jobs. All schedule state is persisted in ctx.storage KV so both jobs survive eviction and hibernation. Sessions are stored in SQLite for atomic expiry queries. The wrangler config uses new_sqlite_classes to correctly provision the SQLite backend.
